name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
۞ أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا۟ قَوْمًا غَضِبَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِم مَّا هُم مِّنكُمْ وَلَا مِنْهُمْ وَيَحْلِفُونَ عَلَى ٱلْكَذِبِ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (١٤)
```

```english
Have you not regarded those who befriend a people at whom Allah is wrathful? They neither belong to you, nor to them, and they swear false oaths [that they are with you] and they know. (14)
```

```arabic
أَعَدَّ ٱللَّهُ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا ۖ إِنَّهُمْ سَآءَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (١٥)
```

```english
Allah has prepared a severe punishment for them. Evil indeed is what they used to do. (15)
```

```arabic
ٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟ أَيْمَـٰنَهُمْ جُنَّةً فَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ (١٦)
```

```english
They make a shield of their oaths and bar [people] from the way of Allah; so there is a humiliating punishment for them. (16)
```

```arabic
لَّن تُغْنِىَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوَٰلُهُمْ وَلَآ أَوْلَـٰدُهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (١٧)
```

```english
Their possessions and children will not avail them in any way against Allah. They shall be the inmates of the Fire and they shall remain in it [forever]. (17)
```

```arabic
يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ جَمِيعًا فَيَحْلِفُونَ لَهُۥ كَمَا يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ ۖ وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍ ۚ أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلْكَـٰذِبُونَ (١٨)
```

```english
The day when Allah will raise them all together, they will swear to Him, just like they swear to you [now], supposing that they stand on something. Look! They are indeed liars! (18)
```

```arabic
ٱسْتَحْوَذَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ فَأَنسَىٰهُمْ ذِكْرَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ حِزْبُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ ۚ أَلَآ إِنَّ حِزْبَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (١٩)
```

```english
Satan has prevailed upon them, so he has caused them to forget the remembrance of Allah. They are Satan’s confederates. Look! Indeed, it is Satan’s confederates who are the losers! (19)
```

```arabic
إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُحَآدُّونَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥٓ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ٱلْأَذَلِّينَ (٢٠)
```

```english
Indeed those who oppose Allah and His Apostle—they will be among the most abased. (20)
```

```arabic
كَتَبَ ٱللَّهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا۠ وَرُسُلِىٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَوِىٌّ عَزِيزٌ (٢١)
```

```english
Allah has ordained: ‘I shall surely prevail, I and My apostles.’ Indeed Allah is all-strong, all-mighty. (21)
```

```arabic
لَّا تَجِدُ قَوْمًا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ يُوَآدُّونَ مَنْ حَآدَّ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَلَوْ كَانُوٓا۟ ءَابَآءَهُمْ أَوْ أَبْنَآءَهُمْ أَوْ إِخْوَٰنَهُمْ أَوْ عَشِيرَتَهُمْ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ كَتَبَ فِى قُلُوبِهِمُ ٱلْإِيمَـٰنَ وَأَيَّدَهُم بِرُوحٍ مِّنْهُ ۖ وَيُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ رَضِىَ ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا۟ عَنْهُ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ حِزْبُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ أَلَآ إِنَّ حِزْبَ ٱللَّهِ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (٢٢)
```

```english
You will not find a people believing in Allah and the Last Day endearing those who oppose Allah and His Apostle even though they were their own parents, or children, or brothers, or kinsfolk. [For] such, He has written faith into their hearts and strengthened them with a spirit from Him. He will admit them into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever], Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. They are Allah’s confederates. Look! The confederates of Allah are indeed felicitous! (22)
```

## (Commentary)

The verses mention a group of hypocrites (المنافقين) who take the Jews as allies and show them affection, while they oppose Allah and His Messenger. The verses condemn them for this, threatening them with a severe punishment and wretchedness. They assert that, in the end, faith (الإيمان) in Allah and the Last Day prevents one from having affection for those who oppose Allah and His Messenger, whoever they may be. Furthermore, the verses praise the believers who disassociate themselves from the enemies of Allah, promising them a stable faith, a spirit (روح) from Allah, Paradise, and His good pleasure.

His saying, the Exalted: **أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا قَوْماً غَضِبَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ** — "Have you not regarded those who have taken for friends a people with whom Allah is at odds?" (58:14). The people with whom Allah is at odds are the Jews. The Exalted said: **مَنْ لَعَنَهُ اللهُ وَغَضِبَ عَلَيْهِ وَجَعَلَ مِنْهُمُ الْقِرَدَةَ وَالْخَنازِيرَ وَعَبَدَ الطَّاغُوتَ** — "those whom Allah has cursed and with whom He is at odds, and some of whom He has made into apes and swine, and worshippers of the Rebel" (5:60).

And His saying: **ما هُمْ مِنْكُمْ وَلا مِنْهُمْ** — "They are neither of you nor of them" (58:14). The pronoun in **عَلَيْهِمْ** (with whom) refers to the hypocrites, and the pronoun in **مِنْهُمْ** (of them) refers to the Jews. The meaning is that these hypocrites, due to their vacillation between disbelief (الكفر) and faith (الإيمان), are neither truly from among you nor from among the Jews. The Exalted said: **مُذَبْذَبِينَ بَيْنَ ذلِكَ لا إِلى هؤُلاءِ وَلا إِلى هؤُلاءِ** — "vacillating between this and that, neither belonging to these nor to those" (4:143).

This is their description according to their outward state. As for the reality, they are joined with those they have taken as allies. The Exalted said: **وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّهُمْ مِنْكُمْ فَإِنَّهُ مِنْهُمْ** — "Any of you who takes them as friends is indeed one of them" (5:51). Thus, there is no contradiction between His saying, "They are neither of you nor of them," and His saying, "is indeed one of them."

Some have suggested that the pronoun in **عَلَيْهِمْ** refers to the "people" (the Jews) and the pronoun in **مِنْهُمْ** refers to the relative pronoun (the hypocrites). The meaning would then be: they took as allies the Jews, who are neither from among you—while you are believers—nor from among these hypocrites themselves; rather, they are outsiders, disassociated from both groups. This carries a type of condemnation, but it is a remote interpretation.

And His saying: **وَيَحْلِفُونَ عَلَى الْكَذِبِ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ** — "and they swear to lies while they know [it]" (58:14). That is, they swear to you upon a lie that they are believers like you, while they know they are lying in their oath.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَعَدَّ اللهُ لَهُمْ عَذاباً شَدِيداً إِنَّهُمْ ساءَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ** — "Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment. Evil indeed is what they used to do" (58:15). Preparation (الإعداد) means to make something ready. His saying, "Evil indeed is..." is the justification for the preparation. In His saying, **كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ** (they used to do), there is an indication that they were persistent in their actions and constant in them.

The meaning is: Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment because of their persistence in their evil deeds.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ جُنَّةً فَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ فَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ مُهِينٌ** — "They make a shield of their oaths, and so they bar [people] from the way of Allah. So there is a humiliating punishment for them" (58:16). Oaths (الأيمان) is the plural of *yamin* (يمين), which is a sworn oath. A shield (الجنة) is a covering used to protect oneself from harm, like a buckler. Humiliating (المهين) is the active participle of *ihana* (إهانة), meaning to debase and disgrace.

The meaning is: they took their oaths as a cover to repel accusation and suspicion from themselves whenever something suspicious to the believers appeared from them. Thus, they turned themselves and others away from the way of Allah (سبيل الله), which is Islam. Therefore, they shall have—for that reason—a debasing and disgraceful punishment.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **لَنْ تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوالُهُمْ وَلا أَوْلادُهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ** — "Neither their wealth nor their children will avail them anything against Allah. They shall be the inmates of the Fire and they shall remain in it [forever]" (58:17). That is, what prompted them toward their current state was the enjoyment of the life of this world, which consists of wealth and children. However, they are in need of escaping an eternal punishment, a need that none can fulfill for them except Allah, the Glorified. They are in poverty toward Him, and neither their wealth nor their children will avail them anything against Him; so let them believe in Him and worship Him.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **يَوْمَ يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللهُ جَمِيعاً فَيَحْلِفُونَ لَهُ كَما يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ عَلى شَيْءٍ** — "The day when Allah will raise them all together, they will swear to Him as they swear to you, and they will suppose that they are on something" (58:18). This is an adverbial phrase for what preceded in His saying, "Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment," or for His saying, "They shall be the inmates of the Fire." His saying, "they will swear to Him as they swear to you," means they will swear to Allah on the Day of Resurrection just as they swear to you in this world.

We have previously explained in the commentary on His saying, the Exalted: **ثُمَّ لَمْ تَكُنْ فِتْنَتُهُمْ إِلَّا أَنْ قالُوا وَاللهِ رَبِّنا ما كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ** — "Then their only excuse will be to say, ‘By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists’" (6:23), that their swearing to a lie on the Day of Resurrection—despite the manifestation of the realities of matters then—is due to the manifestation of their inner dispositions (الملكات) there, because of their rootedness in their souls during the worldly life. They became accustomed in it to presenting falsehood over truth through false oaths. As they live, they die; and as they die, they are resurrected.

Of this nature is their request to be returned to the world on that day, their desire to exit the Fire, their quarreling in the Fire, and other such things recounted by the Noble Quran. They will witness with their own eyes that there is no way to any of that, for that day is a day of recompense, not a day of action.

As for His saying: **وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ عَلى شَيْءٍ** — "and they will suppose that they are on something," it means they think they are established upon something solid that allows them to hide the truth and prevent the manifestation of their lies through denial and false swearing.

This could be a qualification for His saying, "as they swear to you," thus referring to their description in the world—that they think their swearing to you benefits them and satisfies you. In this case, His saying: **أَلا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْكاذِبُونَ** — "Behold! They are indeed the liars" (58:18) is a decree from Him, the Exalted, regarding them, that they are liars; thus, no attention is paid to their nonsense, nor is any weight given to their oaths.

Alternatively, it could be a qualification for His saying, "they will swear to Him," being of the nature of the manifestation of inner dispositions on that day, as mentioned regarding the meaning of their swearing just now. In this case, His saying, "Behold! They are indeed the liars," is a judgment from Him, the Exalted, of their lying on the Day of Resurrection, or absolutely.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **اسْتَحْوَذَ عَلَيْهِمُ الشَّيْطانُ فَأَنْساهُمْ ذِكْرَ اللهِ أُولئِكَ حِزْبُ الشَّيْطانِ أَلا إِنَّ حِزْبَ الشَّيْطانِ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ** — "Satan has prevailed over them and caused them to forget the remembrance of Allah. They are Satan’s confederates. Behold! The confederates of Satan are indeed the losers" (58:19). Prevailing (الاستحواذ) means mastery and dominance. The rest is clear.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحَادُّونَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ أُولئِكَ فِي الْأَذَلِّينَ** — "Indeed those who oppose Allah and His Apostle will be among the most abased" (58:20). This is a justification for their being the losers. That is, they were losers only because they oppose Allah and His Messenger through disobedience and stubbornness; and those who oppose Allah and His Messenger are among the most abased (الأذلين) of Allah's creation.

It is said they are among the most abased because the humiliation of one of two disputants is in proportion to the might (عزة) of the other. Since all might belongs to Allah, nothing remains for the one who opposes Him except pure humiliation.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **كَتَبَ اللهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا وَرُسُلِي إِنَّ اللهَ قَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ** — "Allah has ordained: ‘I shall surely prevail, I and My apostles.’ Indeed Allah is all-strong, all-mighty" (58:21). Ordaining (الكتابة) is the decree (القضاء) from Him, the Exalted.

The apparent generality of "prevailing" (الغلبة) includes prevailing in terms of proof (الحجة), in terms of unseen support (التأييد الغيبي), and in terms of the nature of faith (الإيمان) in Allah and His Messenger.

As for the proof, man is created with the primordial nature (الفطرة) — *the innate disposition toward truth that Allah placed in every human being* — capable of perceiving the truth and submitting to it. If the truth is explained to him through a path he is familiar with, he will soon comprehend it. Once he comprehends it, his primordial nature acknowledges it and his inner self submits to it, even if he does not submit in action due to following passion or any other obstacle.

As for prevailing in terms of unseen support and the decree of truth over falsehood, it is sufficient to consider the types of punishment Allah sent down upon the deniers of past nations, such as the people of Noah, Hud, Salih, Lot, Shu'ayb, the House of Pharaoh, and others to whom He refers by saying: **ثُمَّ أَرْسَلْنا رُسُلَنا تَتْرا كُلَّ ما جاءَ أُمَّةً رَسُولُها كَذَّبُوهُ فَأَتْبَعْنا بَعْضَهُمْ بَعْضاً وَجَعَلْناهُمْ أَحادِيثَ فَبُعْداً لِقَوْمٍ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ** — "Then We sent Our apostles successively. Whenever there came to a nation its apostle, they would impugn him. So We made them follow one another [to destruction] and We made them [the subject of] stories. So away with a people who do not believe!" (23:44). Upon this the divine sunnah (السنة الإلهية) has proceeded, and its mention is summarized in His saying: **وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَسُولٌ فَإِذا جاءَ رَسُولُهُمْ قُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ بِالْقِسْطِ وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ** — "There is an apostle for every nation. So when their apostle comes, judgement is made between them with justice, and they are not wronged" (10:47).

As for prevailing in terms of the nature of faith in Allah and His Messenger, the faith of a believer calls him to defend and protect the truth and to resist falsehood absolutely. He sees that if he kills, he is successful, and if he is killed, he is successful. Thus, his steadfastness in defense is neither conditioned by any condition nor limited by any boundary. This is unlike one who defends not the truth as truth, but rather some worldly objective; he only defends for his own sake. If he sees himself on the brink of destruction or facing great risk, he turns back in flight. He only defends on the condition of, and up to the limit of, personal safety and not being on the verge of death. It is necessary that an absolute resolve prevails over a resolve that is conditioned and limited. A witness to this is the battles of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and the victory and triumph they led to, despite being alternating in fortune; they only ended in the advancement and prevalence of the Muslims.

The Islamic conquests did not cease, nor did the ranks of the Muslims scatter like the people of Saba, except due to the corruption of their intentions and the replacement of the way of godwariness (التقوى) and sincerity to Allah and the spreading of the true religion with the spreading of authority and the expansion of the kingdom. **ذلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللهَ لَمْ يَكُ مُغَيِّراً نِعْمَةً أَنْعَمَها عَلى قَوْمٍ حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا ما بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ** — "That is because Allah never changes a blessing that He has bestowed on a people unless they change what is in their own souls" (8:53). Allah had made it a condition for them, when He perfected their religion and made them secure from their enemy, that they should fear Him, as He said: **الْيَوْمَ يَئِسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ دِينِكُمْ فَلا تَخْشَوْهُمْ وَاخْشَوْنِ** — "Today the faithless have despaired of your religion. So do not fear them, but fear Me" (5:3).

Sufficient to record this prevalence is His saying, the Exalted, addressing the believers: **وَلا تَهِنُوا وَلا تَحْزَنُوا وَأَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ** — "Do not become hollow or mourn, for you are the upper hand, if you are believers" (3:139).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **لا تَجِدُ قَوْماً يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ يُوادُّونَ مَنْ حَادَّ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَلَوْ كانُوا آباءَهُمْ أَوْ أَبْناءَهُمْ أَوْ إِخْوانَهُمْ أَوْ عَشِيرَتَهُمْ** — "You will not find a people believing in Allah and the Last Day love those who oppose Allah and His Apostle, even though they be their fathers, or their sons, or their brothers, or their kinsmen" (58:22). The negation of finding a people with this quality is an allusion to the fact that sincere faith in Allah and the Last Day does not coexist with affection for the people of opposition and stubbornness among the disbelievers, even if it is accompanied by any cause of affection such as fatherhood, sonship, brotherhood, or other types of kinship. Between faith and affection for the people of opposition, there is a contradiction (تضاد) such that they cannot coexist.

It has become clear that His saying, "even though they be their fathers..." is an indication of the causes of affection in general. Kinship affection was specifically mentioned because it is the strongest cause of affection in terms of its stability and lack of change.

And His saying: **أُولئِكَ كَتَبَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْإِيمانَ** — "It is they in whose hearts He has inscribed faith" (58:22). The reference is to the people with the mentioned quality. Inscription (الكتابة) is the firm establishment such that it does not change or vanish. The pronoun refers to Allah, and in this is an explicit statement that they are truly believers.

And His saying: **وَأَيَّدَهُمْ بِرُوحٍ مِنْهُ** — "and whom He has strengthened with a spirit from Him" (58:22). Strengthening (التأييد) is to give power. The pronoun of the subject in **أَيَّدَهُمْ** refers to Allah, the Exalted, as does the pronoun in **مِنْهُ** (from Him), where "from" denotes origin. The meaning is: Allah strengthened them with a spirit from His presence. It is also said that the pronoun refers to faith, meaning: Allah strengthened them with a spirit of the same genus as faith, by which He gives life to their hearts; there is no harm in this interpretation.

It is also said that the spirit refers to Gabriel, or the Quran, or proof and evidence. These are weak views with no evidence from the wording.

Furthermore, the spirit (الروح) — according to what is immediately understood from its meaning — is the principle of life from which power and consciousness emanate. Keeping His saying, "and whom He has strengthened with a spirit from Him," upon its literal meaning implies that the believers possess, beyond the human spirit shared by the believer and the disbeliever, another spirit that pours upon them another life, accompanied by a new power and consciousness. To this, His saying, the Exalted, refers: **أَوَمَنْ كانَ مَيْتاً فَأَحْيَيْناهُ وَجَعَلْنا لَهُ نُوراً يَمْشِي بِهِ فِي النَّاسِ كَمَنْ مَثَلُهُ فِي الظُّلُماتِ لَيْسَ بِخارِجٍ مِنْها** — "Is he who was lifeless, then We gave him life and maintained for him a light by which he walks among the people, like one whose description is that he is in a manifold darkness from which he cannot emerge?" (6:122), and His saying: **مَنْ عَمِلَ صالِحاً مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثى وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَنُحْيِيَنَّهُ حَياةً طَيِّبَةً** — "Whoever acts righteously, [whether] male or female, should he be faithful, We shall surely revive him with a good life" (16:97).

The "goodness of life" mentioned in the verse necessitates the goodness of its effect, which is the power and consciousness from which righteous deeds (الأعمال الصالحة) branch out. These two are expressed in the aforementioned verse of *al-An'am* as "light" (النور), and similar to it is His saying: **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ وَآمِنُوا بِرَسُولِهِ يُؤْتِكُمْ كِفْلَيْنِ مِنْ رَحْمَتِهِ وَيَجْعَلْ لَكُمْ نُوراً تَمْشُونَ بِهِ** — "O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah and believe in His Apostle. He will grant you a double share of His mercy and give you a light to walk by" (57:28).

This is a special, noble life that has specific effects inseparable from man's eternal felicity, beyond the common life shared by the believer and the disbeliever which has common effects. Thus, it has a specific principle, which is the "spirit of faith" (روح الإيمان) mentioned in the verse, beyond the spirit shared by the believer and the disbeliever.

Accordingly, there is no reason for what they mentioned—that the spirit refers to the light of the heart, which is the light of knowledge through which tranquility is attained, and that naming it "spirit" is a trope (مجاز مرسل) because it is the cause of the good eternal life, or a metaphor (استعارة) because in its accompaniment of the modes of knowledge poured upon the heart—and knowledge is the life of the heart just as ignorance is its death—it resembles the spirit that bestows life. [End of quote].

And His saying: **وَيُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها** — "and He will admit them into gardens with conduits running amidst them, to remain in them [forever]" (58:22). This is a beautiful promise and a description of their good afterlife.

And His saying: **رَضِيَ اللهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ** — "Allah is well-pleased with them and they are well-pleased with Him" (58:22). This is an appositive sentence justifying His saying, "and He will admit them into gardens..." Allah's pleasure with them is His mercy toward them for their sincerity in faith to Him, and their pleasure with Him is their delight in what He has provided them of the good life and Paradise.

And His saying: **أُولئِكَ حِزْبُ اللهِ أَلا إِنَّ حِزْبَ اللهِ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ** — "They are Allah’s confederates. Behold! The confederates of Allah are indeed the felicitous" (58:22). This is an honoring of these sincere ones in their faith, by stating that they are His party, just as those hypocrites who ally with the enemies of Allah are the party of Satan; and these are successful, just as those are losers.

In His saying, "Behold! The confederates of Allah," the explicit noun is used in place of the pronoun so that the speech may flow like a well-known proverb.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *al-Majma'*: Regarding His saying, the Exalted: **كَتَبَ اللهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا وَرُسُلِي** — "Allah has ordained: ‘I shall surely prevail, I and My apostles’" (58:21). It is narrated that the Muslims said—when they saw the villages Allah was opening for them: "Allah will surely open for us Rome and Persia." The hypocrites said: "Do you think Persia and Rome are like some of the villages you have overcome?" So Allah sent down this verse.

**I say:** It appears that this is of the nature of applying the verse to the story, and its likes are many. For this reason, it was narrated regarding His saying, the Exalted: **لا تَجِدُ قَوْماً يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ** — "You will not find a people believing in Allah and the Last Day..." (58:22) that it was revealed concerning Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah, who killed his father on the day of Badr. In some narrations: it was revealed concerning Abu Bakr when his father insulted the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم)—so Abu Bakr struck him a blow that he fell to the ground, and the verse was revealed. And concerning Abd al-Rahman ibn Thabit ibn Qays ibn al-Shammas—he asked the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) for permission to visit his maternal uncle from among the polytheists, and he permitted him—when he arrived, the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and the Muslims around him recited the verse.

These are narrations that do not harmonize with the apparent continuity within the verses.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*: al-Tayalisi and Ibn Abi Shaybah narrated from al-Bara' ibn 'Azib who said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: **أوثق عرى الإيمان الحب في الله والبغض في الله** — "The firmest handhold of faith is love for the sake of Allah and hatred for the sake of Allah."

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain to Aban ibn Taghlib from Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) who said: **ما من مؤمن إلا ولقلبه أذنان في جوفه : أذن ينفث فيها الوسواس الخناس ـ وأذن ينفث فيها الملك ـ فيؤيد الله المؤمن بالملك ـ فذلك قوله : وَأَيَّدَهُمْ بِرُوحٍ مِنْهُ** — "There is no believer except that his heart has two ears within it: an ear into which the 'slinking whisperer' (الوسواس الخناس) blows, and an ear into which the angel blows. Thus Allah strengthens the believer with the angel—and that is His saying: 'and whom He has strengthened with a spirit from Him'."

**I say:** Its meaning is not the interpretation of the spirit as the angel; rather, the angel accompanies the spirit and acts through it. The Exalted said: **يُنَزِّلُ الْمَلائِكَةَ بِالرُّوحِ مِنْ أَمْرِهِ** — "He sends down the angels with the spirit by His command" (16:2).

In the same source, with its chain to Ibn Bukayr who said: I said to Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): "When a man commits adultery, the spirit of faith (روح الإيمان) departs from him." He said: **هو قوله : وَأَيَّدَهُمْ بِرُوحٍ مِنْهُ ذلك الذي يفارقه** — "It is His saying: 'and whom He has strengthened with a spirit from Him'; that is what departs from him."

In the same source, with its chain to Muhammad ibn Sinan from Abu Khadijah who said: I entered upon Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) and he said to me: **إن الله تبارك وتعالى أيد المؤمن بروح ـ تحضره في كل وقت يحسن فيه ويتقي ـ وتغيب عنه في كل وقت يذنب فيه ويعتدي ـ فهي معه تهتز سرورا عند إحسانه ـ وتسيخ في الثرى عند إساءته ، فتعاهدوا عباد الله نعمه بإصلاحكم أنفسكم ـ تزدادوا يقينا وتربحوا نفيسا ثمينا ، رحم الله امرءا هم بخير فعمله أو هم بشر فارتدع عنه** — "Indeed Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, has strengthened the believer with a spirit—it is present with him at every time he does good and is godwary, and it is absent from him at every time he sins and transgresses. It is with him, vibrating with joy at his good-doing, and sinking into the earth at his evil-doing. So, O servants of Allah, tend to His blessings by reforming yourselves—you will increase in certainty and gain something precious and valuable. May Allah have mercy on a person who intended good and performed it, or intended evil and refrained from it." Then he said: **نحن نؤيد الروح بالطاعة لله والعمل له** — "We strengthen the spirit through obedience to Allah and action for His sake."

**I say:** It has become clear from what preceded in the commentary on the verse that this spirit is among the levels of the human spirit attained by the believer when he perfects his faith. Thus, it is not separate from him, just as the vegetative, animal, and human spirits shared by the believer and the disbeliever are levels of his spirit and not separate from him. However, it begins as a good state (هيئة) in the soul that may vanish due to the occurrence of an evil state that opposes it, then returns when the opposing obstacles vanish, until it becomes established, rooted, and the soul is formed by it; then it remains firm and does not change.

By this, it becomes clear that the meaning of the Imam's (peace be upon him) saying, "a spirit that is present with him," and his saying, "it is with him," refers to the presence of its form as an accidental state capable of vanishing. His saying, "sinking into the earth," refers to the vanishing of the state by way of metaphor. Likewise is the saying of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) in the previous narration: "the spirit of faith departs from him."
